message:
fix(operators): align step tip keys with actual step IDs

The documentation stepTips keys didn't match the actual step IDs from
operator implementations, causing getStepTip() to return undefined.

Fixed Level Split:
- generate-x-levels → identify-x-levels
- generate-y-levels → identify-y-levels
- generate-combinations → review-level-matrix
- record-levels → choose-focus
- Added missing generate-sub-hypotheses

Fixed Object Transpose:
- review-hypothesis → state-hypothesis
- generate-discriminating-tests → identify-tests

Fixed Scale Check:
- input-effect → quantify-effect
- compare-context → contextualize-scale
- check-measurement → measurement-precision
- assess-practical → practical-significance
- Added missing population-individual

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"brenner_bot-nu8g","title":"EPIC: Artifact Generation - Research Briefs & Exports","description":"# Artifact Generation - Research Briefs & Exports

## Purpose
Transform ephemeral sessions into durable, shareable research artifacts that capture the intellectual work done.

## Why Artifacts Matter
The value of the Brenner Loop isn't just the thinking—it's the **record of the thinking**. Good artifacts:
1. Survive beyond the session
2. Can be shared with collaborators
3. Serve as institutional memory
4. Document the intellectual journey, not just conclusions

## Core Artifact Types

### 1. Research Brief
The primary output. A structured document containing:
- Executive Summary (2-3 sentences)
- Hypothesis Statement (current version)
- Hypothesis Evolution (how it changed and why)
- Discriminative Tests Identified (ranked by power)
- Agent Analysis (consensus and conflicts)
- Evidence Summary (if any tests completed)
- Brenner Principles Applied (which quotes, which operators)
- Recommended Next Steps

Format: Markdown with structured metadata header.

### 2. Hypothesis Card (Export)
Standalone export of a hypothesis with:
- Full card structure
- Version history
- Robustness score
- Confounds identified

Format: JSON (for import/programmatic use) or Markdown (for reading).

### 3. Evidence Ledger Export
Complete audit trail of:
- All tests recorded
- Confidence updates over time
- Sources and observations

Format: CSV (for analysis) or Markdown (for review).

### 4. Session Transcript
Full record of the session including:
- All operator applications
- All agent responses
- All user decisions
- Timeline of events

Format: Markdown with timestamps.

## Structured Output Schema

### Research Brief Metadata
```yaml
---
type: research_brief
version: 1.0
session_id: SM-2024-001
hypothesis_id: hyp-xyz
created_at: 2024-03-15T10:30:00Z
confidence: 37%
status: under_pressure
operators_applied:
  - level_split
  - exclusion_test
agents_consulted:
  - devils_advocate
  - experiment_designer
  - brenner_channeler
tests_identified: 5
tests_completed: 2
brenner_citations:
  - §42
  - §89
  - §73
---
```

### Export Formats
- **PDF**: For sharing with non-technical audiences
- **Markdown**: For git storage, collaboration
- **JSON**: For programmatic use, import/export
- **HTML**: For web viewing with interactivity

## Citation Management
Research Briefs should properly cite:
1. Brenner transcript sections (§42, §89, etc.)
2. External sources added to evidence ledger
3. Agent responses (which agent, which session)

## Templates
Users may want different Brief styles:
- **Executive Summary**: 1-page version for stakeholders
- **Technical Detail**: Full version with all data
- **Presentation Mode**: Slides-friendly structure
- **Academic Format**: Citation-ready for papers

## Dependencies
- All other components (aggregates their outputs)

## Success Criteria
- Research Briefs are genuinely useful for sharing
- Exports work across formats without data loss
- Citations are complete and verifiable
- Users actually export and share artifacts

-{"id":"brenner_bot-nu8g.1","title":"Design Research Brief Template","description":"# Design Research Brief Template

## Purpose
Create the template and structure for the primary output artifact: the Research Brief.

## Research Brief Sections

### 1. Metadata Header
```yaml
---
type: research_brief
version: 1.0
session_id: SM-2024-001
hypothesis_id: hyp-xyz
created_at: 2024-03-15T10:30:00Z
final_confidence: 37%
status: under_pressure
---
```

### 2. Executive Summary
2-3 sentences capturing:
- The hypothesis investigated
- Key finding (supported/challenged/unclear)
- Primary recommendation

### 3. Hypothesis Statement
Current formulation with:
- Statement
- Mechanism
- Domain

### 4. Hypothesis Evolution
If hypothesis changed during session:
- Original → Current transformation
- Trigger for each change
- Visual evolution diagram (optional)

### 5. Discriminative Structure
- Predictions if true
- Predictions if false
- Falsification conditions

### 6. Operators Applied
For each operator used:
- What was discovered
- Key outputs

### 7. Agent Analysis
- Devil's Advocate findings
- Experiment Designer protocols
- Brenner Channeler wisdom
- Consensus and conflicts

### 8. Evidence Summary
If evidence recorded:
- Tests run
- Results
- Confidence trajectory

### 9. Brenner Principles
Which transcript sections were relevant:
- §42: Level Split
- §89: Exclusion Test
- etc.

### 10. Recommended Next Steps
Prioritized actions:
1. High priority: [action]
2. Medium priority: [action]
3. etc.

## Template Engine
Use a template system that can:
- Fill from session data
- Handle missing sections gracefully
- Export to multiple formats

## File Location
`apps/web/src/lib/brenner-loop/artifacts/research-brief-template.ts`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All sections defined
- [ ] Template handles missing data
- [ ] Metadata schema complete
- [ ] Template readable and useful

 {"id":"brenner_bot-nu8g.2","title":"Implement Multi-Format Export (Markdown, PDF, JSON)","description":"# Implement Multi-Format Export (Markdown, PDF, JSON)

## Purpose
Allow users to export Research Briefs in multiple formats for different use cases.

## Export Formats

### Markdown (.md)
- Primary format
- Git-friendly
- Human-readable
- Can convert to other formats

```typescript
function exportToMarkdown(brief: ResearchBrief): string;
```

### PDF (.pdf)
- For sharing with non-technical audiences
- Print-friendly layout
- Professional appearance

Options:
- Browser print-to-PDF (simplest)
- Library like jsPDF or react-pdf
- Server-side generation with puppeteer (future)

```typescript
async function exportToPDF(brief: ResearchBrief): Promise<Blob>;
```

### JSON (.json)
- For programmatic use
- Import/export between sessions
- API responses

```typescript
function exportToJSON(brief: ResearchBrief): string;
```

### HTML (.html)
- Self-contained web view
- Email-friendly
- Interactive elements preserved (optional)

```typescript
function exportToHTML(brief: ResearchBrief): string;
```

## UI for Export
```
┌─────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Export Research Brief              │
├─────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                     │
│  Format:                            │
│  ○ Markdown (.md) - Recommended     │
│  ○ PDF (.pdf)                       │
│  ○ JSON (.json)                     │
│  ○ HTML (.html)                     │
│                                     │
│  Options:                           │
│  □ Include full agent responses     │
│  □ Include evidence details         │
│  □ Include evolution graph          │
│                                     │
│  [Cancel] [Export]                  │
│                                     │
└─────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## File Location
`apps/web/src/lib/brenner-loop/artifacts/export.ts`
`apps/web/src/components/brenner-loop/artifacts/ExportModal.tsx`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Markdown export works perfectly
- [ ] PDF export produces readable document
- [ ] JSON export round-trips correctly
- [ ] UI provides clear options

 {"id":"brenner_bot-nu8g.3","title":"Build Citation Manager for Brenner References","description":"# Build Citation Manager for Brenner References

## Purpose
Properly cite Brenner transcript sections and external sources throughout artifacts.

## Citation Types

### 1. Brenner Transcript Citations
Format: §42, §89, §107, etc.

Each citation should link to:
- The transcript section
- A popup/tooltip with the quote
- Context in distillations if available

```typescript
interface BrennerCitation {
  section: number;
  quote: string;
  context?: string;
  operatorRelevance?: OperatorType[];
}
```

### 2. External Source Citations
User-added sources from Evidence Ledger:

```typescript
interface ExternalCitation {
  id: string;
  type: 'paper' | 'article' | 'book' | 'dataset' | 'observation' | 'other';
  title: string;
  authors?: string;
  year?: number;
  url?: string;
  doi?: string;
  notes?: string;
}
```

## Citation in Research Briefs
Each mention of Brenner insight should be cited:

```markdown
As Brenner noted, "What you need is an observation that would be
IMPOSSIBLE if you're wrong. Not merely unlikely — impossible" (§89).
```

## Citation Index
At the end of Research Brief:

```markdown
## References

### Brenner Transcript
- §42: Level separation principle
- §89: Falsification requirement
- §73: Chastity control concept

### External Sources
- Smith et al. (2023). "Social Media and Teen Mental Health." Journal...
- Instagram Algorithm Change Dataset (2021). Retrieved from...
```

## File Location
`apps/web/src/lib/brenner-loop/artifacts/citations.ts`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Brenner citations link to transcript
- [ ] External citations properly formatted
- [ ] Citation index generated
- [ ] Multiple formats supported

 {"id":"brenner_bot-o546","title":"TASK: Surface Relevant Corpus Quotes in Operator Sessions","description":"
## Make the Corpus ALIVE During Sessions

The Brenner corpus contains wisdom for every operator. During Level Split, show Brenner quotes about levels of explanation. During Exclusion Test, show quotes about designing tests.

### Integration Points

**Level Split (Σ) Operator**
- Surface quotes about "levels of explanation"
- Brenner on reductionism vs. holism
- Examples of level splitting from his work

**Exclusion Test (⊘) Operator**
- Quotes about discriminative tests
- "The test must be able to give a clean answer"
- Examples of tests that excluded alternatives

**Object Transpose (⟳) Operator**
- Quotes about changing reference frames
- Looking at problems from different angles
- His examples of productive transpositions

**Scale Check (⊙) Operator**
- Quotes about scale-dependence
- When effects change with scale
- His examples of scale-checking insights

### Implementation
- Use semantic search (ukd1.1) to find relevant quotes
- Query: [current hypothesis text] + [operator name]
- Show 2-3 most relevant quotes in sidebar
- Quote attribution with link to full transcript

### Quote Display
- Collapsible "Brenner Says" panel
- Quote text with context
- "Read more" link to corpus location
- User can favorite/bookmark quotes

### Depends On
- ukd1.1 (Vector Embeddings for Brenner Corpus)

- Validates session kickoff form data
- Creates initial session artifacts
- Sets up research thread and initial hypotheses
- Handles operator selection and roster assignment

## Scope
- Review initialization logic for edge cases
- Check form validation completeness
- Verify proper error handling
- Check test coverage
- Fix obvious bugs (no deletions per AGENTS.md)

## Complements prior audits
This pairs with artifact-merge and delta-parser audits, covering the session creation layer.

## Acceptance
- All identified bugs documented or fixed
- Test coverage verified/improved","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonValley","created_at":"2026-01-01T20:52:33.961673556-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-01T20:54:45.862859087-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-01T20:54:45.862859087-05:00","close_reason":"AUDIT COMPLETE: session-kickoff.ts (504 lines) - NO BUGS FOUND. -{"id":"brenner_bot-w5p6","title":"Tutorial Path: Agent-Assisted Research (8 Steps)","description":"# Purpose

Implement the Agent-Assisted tutorial path — 8 steps that teach users how to have their AI coding agent (Claude Code or Codex) study the BrennerBot system and then run the Brenner Loop for them.

This is the **highest-leverage path**. Once an AI agent internalizes the methodology, it can apply it repeatedly to different research questions. The human provides domain expertise and oversight; the AI provides methodological discipline.

---

# The Core Insight

**AI agents can read documentation and internalize methodology.**

When you have Claude Code or Codex:
1. Clone the brenner_bot repo
2. Read AGENTS.md, README.md, and the distillations
3. Explore the operator library and specs

...the agent doesn't just learn commands. It learns to THINK with Brenner's cognitive toolkit.

---

# Tool-Specific Guidance (IMPORTANT)

**Claude Code and Codex have different interfaces.** While the prompts are largely the same, we need:

1. **Claude Code Tab** (for Claude Max users)
   - Uses `claude` command
   - Project context via --project flag
   - Opus 4.5 model

2. **Codex Tab** (for GPT Pro users)
   - Uses `codex` command  
   - Project context works similarly
   - GPT 5.2 model

**Implementation**: Use the same tab component as Quick Start's OS-specific instructions. Show tool-specific setup and verification, but keep the core prompts unified.

---

# Prerequisites

**Required (one of)**:
- Claude Code with Opus 4.5 (Claude Max subscription)
- Codex CLI with GPT 5.2 (GPT Pro subscription)

**Also needed**:
- Basic familiarity with the CLI agent interface
- Git and terminal basics

---

# Step-by-Step Breakdown

## Step 1: Why Agent-Assisted? (~3 min)
Explain the leverage, set expectations, clarify human-AI collaboration model.

## Step 2: Prerequisites (~5 min)
**Tool-specific tabs here**: Verify Claude Code OR Codex installed, check subscription tier.

+{"id":"brenner_bot-w5p6","title":"Tutorial Path: Agent-Assisted Research (8 Steps)","description":"# Purpose

Implement the Agent-Assisted tutorial path — 8 steps that teach users how to have their AI coding agent (Claude Code or Codex) study the BrennerBot system and then run the Brenner Loop for them.

This is the **highest-leverage path**. Once an AI agent internalizes the methodology, it can apply it repeatedly to different research questions. The human provides domain expertise and oversight; the AI provides methodological discipline.

---

# The Core Insight

**AI agents can read documentation and internalize methodology.**

When you have Claude Code or Codex:
1. Clone the brenner_bot repo
2. Read AGENTS.md, README.md, and the distillations
3. Explore the operator library and specs

...the agent doesn't just learn commands. It learns to THINK with Brenner's cognitive toolkit.

---

# Tool-Specific Guidance (IMPORTANT)

**Claude Code and Codex have different interfaces.** While the prompts are largely the same, we need:

1. **Claude Code Tab** (for Claude Max users)
   - Uses `claude` command
   - Project context via --project flag
   - Opus 4.5 model

2. **Codex Tab** (for GPT Pro users)
   - Uses `codex` command  
   - Project context works similarly
   - GPT 5.2 model

**Implementation**: Use the same tab component as Quick Start's OS-specific instructions. Show tool-specific setup and verification, but keep the core prompts unified.

---

# Prerequisites

**Required (one of)**:
- Claude Code with Opus 4.5 (Claude Max subscription)
- Codex CLI with GPT 5.2 (GPT Pro subscription)

**Also needed**:
- Basic familiarity with the CLI agent interface
- Git and terminal basics

---

# Step-by-Step Breakdown

## Step 1: Why Agent-Assisted? (~3 min)
Explain the leverage, set expectations, clarify human-AI collaboration model.

## Step 2: Prerequisites (~5 min)
**Tool-specific tabs here**: Verify Claude Code OR Codex installed, check subscription tier.

## Step 3: Clone into Agent Context (~5 min)
Have the agent clone brenner_bot, verify file access.

## Step 4: Agent Studies the System (~10 min) ⭐ KEY STEP
**Uses prompts from Prompt Templates Library** (brenner_bot-u38r)

Agent reads:
- AGENTS.md, README.md
- specs/operator_library_v0.1.md
- At least one distillation

Verification: Agent's summary should mention key operators.

## Step 5: Define Your Research Problem (~5 min)
User provides their question, agent helps refine using Brenner criteria.

## Step 6: Agent Builds the Inputs (~10 min)
Agent searches corpus, composes excerpt, drafts kickoff prompt.
Human checkpoint: User reviews inputs before proceeding.

## Step 7: Agent Runs the Brenner Loop (~10 min)
Agent produces structured artifact with all sections.
Output: ~2000-4000 word research artifact.

## Step 8: Human Review (~5 min)
**Interactive checklist** for reviewing agent output:
- Hypothesis quality checks
- Test quality checks
- Assumption completeness
- Critique substantiveness

Common failure modes documented with fixes.

---

# Critical Dependencies

- brenner_bot-u38r (Prompt Templates Library) - The prompts ARE the interface
- brenner_bot-8i1c (Domain Examples) - Users need to see "good" artifacts to compare

---

# Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All 8 steps implemented with full content
- [ ] Tool-specific tabs for Claude Code vs Codex
- [ ] Prompts link to Prompt Templates Library
- [ ] Review checklist is interactive
- [ ] Troubleshooting for common agent failures
- [ ] Human oversight emphasized throughout","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T14:52:04.355522747-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-04T16:07:24.240563182-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-w5p6","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-do3j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T14:54:04.502582177-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-wcr8","title":"Tutorial Component: TutorialProgress (step indicator sidebar)","description":"# Component: TutorialProgress

The progress indicator that shows users where they are in the tutorial path.

## Behavior

**Desktop (width >= 768px)**:
- Vertical list on left sidebar
- Fixed position, scrolls with page
- Shows all steps with numbers
- Current step highlighted with primary accent
- Completed steps show checkmark + muted color
- Clickable to navigate (if allowed)

**Mobile (width < 768px)**:
- Horizontal progress bar at top
- Minimal text, mostly visual
- "Step X of Y" label
- Collapses to icon on scroll

## Props
\`\`\`typescript
interface TutorialProgressProps {
  steps: Array<{ id: string; title: string; estimatedTime: string }>;
  currentStep: number;
  completedSteps: number[];
  onStepClick?: (stepIndex: number) => void;
  allowJumpAhead?: boolean;
}
\`\`\`

## Tests
- Renders all steps
- Highlights current step
- Shows checkmarks for completed
- Click navigation works when allowed
- Mobile breakpoint layout","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T14:54:53.679913239-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-04T13:34:09.443698194-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T13:34:09.443698194-05:00","close_reason":"Already implemented: TutorialProgress.tsx exists with 29KB of code including desktop sidebar, mobile dots, swipe gestures, animations, and all specified features","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-wcr8","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-thzu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T14:55:41.743176235-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}

 {"id":"brenner_bot-wpb","title":"Create OperatorSymbol component with tooltip","description":"# Task

Create a distinctive inline component for Brenner operator symbols.

# The Operators

⊘ Level-split | 𝓛 Recode | ≡ Invariant-extract | ✂ Exclusion-test
⟂ Object-transpose | ↑ Amplify | ⊕ Cross-domain | ◊ Paradox-hunt
ΔE Exception-quarantine | ∿ Dephase | † Theory-kill | ⌂ Materialize
🔧 DIY | ⊞ Scale-check

# Design

Each operator symbol should:
1. Stand out from surrounding text (monospace font, slightly larger)
2. Have a subtle background pill
3. Show tooltip on hover with name + definition
4. Link to the operator palette page

# Visual

```
The ⊘ Level-split operator separates...
    ^
    └── pill background, hover shows tooltip
```

# Tooltip Content

- Operator name
- One-line definition
- 'Learn more →' link

# Props

```tsx
interface OperatorSymbolProps {
  symbol: '⊘' | '𝓛' | '≡' | '✂' | '⟂' | '↑' | '⊕' | '◊' | 'ΔE' | '∿' | '†' | '⌂' | '🔧' | '⊞';
  showName?: boolean;  // Show name alongside symbol
  linkToPage?: boolean; // Make it a link to operator page
}
```

# Operator Data

Create a data file with all operator definitions:
```typescript
export const OPERATORS = {
  '⊘': { name: 'Level-split', definition: 'Separate program from interpreter...' },
  // ...
};
```

# Animation

- Subtle scale on hover
- Tooltip fade in

# Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All 14 operators render correctly
- [ ] Tooltips show on hover
- [ ] Links work if enabled
- [ ] Accessible (keyboard tooltip trigger)
- [ ] Works inline with text","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:22:13.719833091-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T19:29:11.392954222-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-29T19:29:11.392954222-05:00","close_reason":"Folded into brenner_bot-5so.4.5.2 (operator palette UI)","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-wpb","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-8yn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:23:06.878775514-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
